description: Backend-developer handover manifest. Machine-readable index of front-end elements that are mock / visual-only / in-memory and need real backend implementation. Front-end-only repo (vanilla HTML/CSS/JS, no framework, no backend). See HANDOVER.md for the human-readable version and the TODO(backend:<Surface>) in-code marker convention.

categories.visual-only: Rendered but no behaviour wired at all.
categories.fe-wired: Works client-side; no backend required (may be fine as-is).
categories.needs-backend: Requires real data, API, auth, persistence, or AI service.

ControlHub.note: CC filterable directory screen. Reuses the ControlScreen app-shell (see the ControlScreen surface for all shell-level backend items — sidebar nav, auth, notifications, AI assistant, favourites, etc.). The items below are specific to ControlHub's own page content; everything else is inherited from the reused shell.

items.current: Filter field renders (search icon + clear button) but no filtering logic is wired — typing does nothing; the clear button is the component default only.
items.needs: Client-side filtering is sufficient (no backend): debounced text match over ActionCard titles across all sections, hiding cards (and empty section headings) that don't match; clear button resets. Promote to fe-wired once implemented.

items.element: .action-card--chevron (href="#") + .action-card--button (whole-card nav, pointer cursor) with a separate .btn ("+ add")
items.current: Both card variants are meant to navigate the whole card to the area's screen. Chevron cards link to '#'; Button cards show a pointer cursor but card-level navigation isn't wired; the 'add' buttons are inert.
items.needs: Real navigation targets per area for BOTH variants (whole card → the corresponding report/analysis screen — implement the Button card as a stretched-link so the '+ add' button stays a separate action above it) and an add-to-favourites/dashboard action for the 'add' button (POST, then reflect added state). Card title + section taxonomy comes from the same content source as the sidebar nav.

ControlScreen.note: CC home/landing dashboard. Front-end complete; all data is hardcoded and most actions are mock. Composes many CC patterns — see docs/component-registry.md for per-component status.

items.current: Saving/removing a favourite only mutates the DOM in memory; lost on reload. Mirrored across both list copies.
items.needs: Favourites CRUD API: GET (load on init), POST (save {name, context, timestamp}), DELETE. Replace in-memory DOM mutation with API-backed state.

items.current: Pinning a nav sub-item floats it to the top (order:-1) and fills the pin icon, but state is in-memory only — held on the DOM, scoped to one menu tree (not synced desktop↔mobile) and lost on reload.
items.needs: Persist pinned sub-items via a user-prefs API: GET pinned ids on init (apply --pinned on render), POST on pin, DELETE on unpin. Key by user + menu item id; share state across the desktop and mobile menu trees.
Per-panel search/filter API (debounced query → results), populate the lists from the response.

items.element: Static submenu <li> links in the SidebarMenu panels (CRM/Content/Marketing/etc.)
items.current: Hardcoded list items; no navigation, no permission filtering.
items.needs: Menu/structure API to populate items; permission-based visibility; real navigation/routing on click.

items.element: Zone Selector dropdown (.sel, wired by Select.js for open/close only)
items.needs: Load available zones; persist selection to session; reload dashboard data for the chosen zone.

items.element: 2 Chart.js charts (Page Views, Users) + time-range buttons + report links
items.current: Hardcoded data arrays + static labels; range buttons and report links are visual only.
items.needs: Analytics API for series data; time-range query (7d/30d/etc.) updating the chart; real routes for Clicks/Revenue reports.

items.element: Datatables 'Who's Online' (desktop + mobile) — rows, search, sort headers, pagination
items.current: Static rows (5 fake users); search/sort/pagination controls are visual only.
items.needs: Users API with server-side paginate/sort/search; live 'online' status (poll or WebSocket); touch/login metadata from auth.

items.element: Logout / Sign Out (user menu) / Switch Mode (dev→prod alert CTA) / View Site
items.current: No click handlers (Switch Mode + View Site + header Logout); Sign Out visual only.
items.needs: Auth/session endpoints (logout → clear session → redirect), mode-switch endpoint, View Site navigation.
Notifications API (count + list) and a drawer/page on click.

items.element: Mounted AiAssistant panel — message input, chat history list, pin/copy/delete menu
items.current: Initial-state view only: send button is inert, chat history is static, menu actions are visual. Processing/response states are demo-only (GSAP) on the standalone AiAssistant page, NOT mounted here. (Distinct from the future full-page CC AI Chat the Maximise button will route to.)
items.needs: Chat/AI backend: send message + stream response, chat-history CRUD (load/pin/delete), thumbs-up/down feedback. Session association with the user.

items.current: Emits input/change events only; not bound to any font-scale variable.
items.needs: Bind to a font-scale CSS var site-wide and persist the preference per user.
Toggle a condensed spacing view — apply data-layout="minimised" on the shell (minimised-mode tokens already exist in css/tokens-minimised.css) — and persist the preference per user.
None required. Only if a server-rendered print-to-PDF is wanted later.
